headline: Mexico Commits 58 Billion Pesos to School Infrastructure in 2026
meta_description: Mexico announces its largest federal school investment ever, with 58 billion pesos dedicated to educational infrastructure and support for over 71,000 schools.
summary_short: Mexico is investing record funding into school infrastructure this year, with a program already supporting tens of thousands of educational facilities across the country.
body:
Mexico is making its largest federal school investment ever, committing 58 billion pesos to educational infrastructure in 2026. The move signals a major push to strengthen the country's public education system from the ground up.

The initiative builds on progress from La Escuela es Nuestra, a program that has already extended support to 71,482 schools nationwide. By channeling substantial resources directly into infrastructure, Mexico is addressing long-standing gaps in classroom quality, facility maintenance, and learning environments that affect millions of students.

The scale of this investment reflects a growing recognition that sustainable educational improvement requires sustained funding. With over 71,000 schools already participating in support programs, Mexico's commitment demonstrates a systematic effort to reach schools across diverse regions and communities, from urban centers to remote areas.
quick_facts: 58 billion pesos allocated for school infrastructure in 2026
La Escuela es Nuestra supports 71,482 schools nationwide
Mexico's largest federal education investment to date
